segments.text: It was just before the Passover Festival. Jesus knew that the hour had come for him to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.
The evening meal was in progress, and the devil had already prompted Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, to betray Jesus.
Jesus knew that the Father had put all things under his power, and that he had come from God and was returning to God;
so he got up from the meal, took off his outer clothing, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
After that, he poured water into a basin and began to wash his disciples' feet, drying them with the towel that was wrapped around him.
He came to Simon Peter, who
Lord, are you going to wash my feet?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Jesus replied,
You do not realize now what I am doing, but later you will understand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No, you shall never wash my feet.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Unless I wash you, you have no part with me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then, Lord, not just my feet but my hands and my head as well!
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Those who have had a bath need only to wash their feet; their whole body is clean. And you are clean, though not every one of you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For he knew who was going to betray him, and that was why he said not every one was clean.
When he had finished washing their feet, he put on his clothes and returned to his place.
Do you understand what I have done for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You call me 'Teacher' and 'Lord,' and rightly so, for that is what I am.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now that I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also should wash one another's feet.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I have set you an example that you should do as I have done for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Very truly I tell you, no servant is greater than his master, nor is a messenger greater than the one who sent him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now that you know these things, you will be blessed if you do them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am not referring to all of you; I know those I have chosen. But this is to fulfill this passage of Scripture: 'He who shared my bread has turned against me.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am telling you now before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I am who I am.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Very truly I tell you, whoever accepts anyone I send accepts me; and whoever accepts me accepts the one who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After he had said this, Jesus was troubled in spirit and testified,
Very truly I tell you, one of you is going to betray me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His disciples stared at one another, at a loss to know which of them he meant.
One of them, the disciple whom Jesus loved, was reclining next to him.
Simon Peter motioned to this disciple and said,
Ask him which one he means.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Lord, who is it?
segments.speaker: Disciple Jesus Loved

segments.text: It is the one to whom I will give this piece of bread when I have dipped it in the dish.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then, dipping the piece of bread, he gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot.
What you are about to do, do quickly.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But no one at the meal understood why Jesus said this to him.
Since Judas had charge of the money, some thought Jesus was telling him to buy what was needed for the festival, or to give something to the poor.
As soon as Judas had taken the bread, he went out. And it was night.
Now the Son of Man is glorified and God is glorified in him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If God is glorified in him, God will glorify the Son in himself, and will glorify him at once.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: My children, I will be with you only a little longer. You will look for me, and just as I told the Jews, so I tell you now: Where I am going, you cannot come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A new command I give you: Love one another. As I have loved you, so you must love one another.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, where are you going?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Where I am going, you cannot follow now, but you will follow later.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, why can't I follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Will you really lay down your life for me? Very truly I tell you, before the rooster crows, you will disown me three times!
segments.speaker: Jesus
